incorporations.reason: Italian 'mirasse' is an imperfect subjunctive expressing hypothetical intent; 'might seek' conveys that tentativeness better than the more prescriptive 'should aim'

incorporations.original: so distant, in time and in space, from the events he recounts
incorporations.replacement: so far removed, in time and in space, from the events he narrates
incorporations.reason: 'Far removed' better renders 'tanto lontano' as a compound intensifier; 'narrates' is a closer match for 'narra' than 'recounts'
Edgren gives 'affatto' as 'perfectly; entirely; quite'; 'altogether' captures the emphatic totality of 'affatto' more idiomatically in this construction
'Farthest' better captures the spatial sense of 'estremo' (Edgren: 'extreme, utmost') when referring to geographic remoteness, and avoids the modern connotation of 'extreme' as radical
A clean English rendering is preferable in running prose; the bracketed gloss interrupted the rhetorical flow of this impassioned passage

incorporations.original: to find a specimen of that epoch
incorporations.replacement: to find an exemplar of that epoch
incorporations.reason: 'Exemplar' more faithfully renders 'esemplare', which implies a representative or model, not a clinical 'specimen'

incorporations.original: my existence was thereby enriched with edifying and pleasurable sensations
incorporations.replacement: my existence was thereby intensified by edifying and pleasurable sensations
incorporations.reason: Italian 'intensificata' literally means 'intensified'; 'enriched' is an interpretive softening that loses Crespi's precise word

incorporations.original: In the surge of men and events that welled forth from the European revolution
incorporations.replacement: In the great outpouring of men and events that sprang from the European revolution
incorporations.reason: 'Rigurgito' literally means a surging back up or disgorging; 'great outpouring' conveys the overwhelming volume and force while remaining natural English

incorporations.original: My sympathies are with those who succumb with virility.
incorporations.replacement: My sympathies are for those who manfully succumb.
incorporations.reason: 'Virilmente soccombe' places the adverb before the verb; 'manfully succumb' mirrors this word order and is more concise than the prepositional 'succumb with virility'

incorporations.original: I surmised that this book would see the light
incorporations.replacement: I sensed that this book would see the light
incorporations.reason: 'Intuivo' denotes intuition or sensing, not logical inference; 'sensed' is a more accurate rendering than 'surmised'

incorporations.original: I not only sought and obtained written approval of each individual chapter; but I asked as well, and obtained
incorporations.replacement: not only did I procure the approval, in writing, of every single chapter, but I also requested and obtained
incorporations.reason: Gemini's inverted construction ('not only did I') better mirrors the emphatic Italian 'non solo provocai' and reads more naturally in English literary prose; 'procure' is a closer match for 'provocai' in this legal-documentary sense

incorporations.original: I might have softened a certain dissonance, rendered the more jarring
incorporations.replacement: I might have softened certain jarring notes, rendered the more strident
incorporations.reason: Italian 'qualche stonatura, fatta più stridente' — 'stonatura' means a wrong note or jarring note, not an abstract 'dissonance'; 'strident' is the direct cognate of 'stridente'

incorporations.original: certain heterogeneous marks that insinuated themselves
incorporations.replacement: certain heterogeneous imprints that insinuated themselves
incorporations.reason: 'Impronta' means imprint or stamp, not merely a mark; 'imprints' is the direct cognate and preserves the metaphor of an author's impression left on the text

incorporations.original: whom di Rudio held most dear
incorporations.reason: 'Dilesse' (passato remoto of 'diligere') means to cherish or hold in special esteem; 'most cherished' is more concise and closer to the Italian verb form

edgren_influences.note: Edgren confirms 'rummage' as the period-standard translation of 'rovistando'; this supported retaining the Claude draft's 'rummaging' over Gemini's 'delving into', which loses the physical, searching quality of the original

Edgren lists 'perfectly; entirely; quite' for 'affatto', confirming it as an intensifier meaning 'completely' (not the modern Italian false-friend sense of 'not at all'); 'altogether' was chosen from the Gemini draft as a strong English intensifier in this adjectival position

Edgren gives 'extreme, utmost' — in a geographic context ('estremo Occidente'), 'farthest' captures the spatial superlative sense of 'utmost' and avoids the modern connotation of 'extreme' as radical or excessive

The Edgren entry for 'stillante' is fragmentary, but the root 'stillare' means to distil or drip; this confirmed 'distilling' (from the Claude draft) as period-appropriate over Gemini's 'exuding', preserving the image of poetry being slowly extracted drop by drop

Edgren gives 'misery (wretchedness, extreme poverty, stinginess)' — this confirmed the literal 'miseries' as appropriate for 'miserie della politica', rather than softening to 'woes' or 'troubles'; both drafts agreed here
